name: Osso Buco
recipeInstructions:
1. Season the veal shanks with salt and pepper. Dredge each shank in flour, shaking off excess.
2. Heat 3 tablespoons butter and 3 tablespoons olive oil in a heavy skillet over medium-high heat. Brown the veal shanks, adding more butter and oil if needed. Transfer browned shanks to a platter.
3. Reduce wine mixture to about half a cup. Set aside in a small bowl.
4. In a flameproof casserole dish large enough to hold the veal shanks in one layer, cook onion, carrots, celery, and garlic in remaining 4 tablespoons butter over medium-low heat, stirring occasionally until vegetables are softened.
5. Add browned shanks and any accumulated juices to the casserole. Add wine mixture and enough broth to just cover the shanks. Scatter tomatoes over shanks, add bouquet garni (6 fresh parsley sprigs, 4 fresh thyme sprigs, 1 bay leaf), and season with salt and pepper.
6. Bring liquid to a simmer over moderately high heat. Braise covered in preheated 325°F oven for 2 hours or until veal is tender.
7. Transfer veal to an ovenproof serving dish using a slotted spoon. Discard bouquet garni. Strain pan juices into a saucepan, pressing solids. Skim fat. Reduce by boiling for about 15 minutes until reduced to about 3 cups.
8. Baste shanks several times with the reduced juices and return them to the oven for 10 more basting sessions or until glazed. Sprinkle gremolata over shanks and serve remaining sauce alongside.
